subtopics.description: Current role title and primary responsibilities
coverage_criteria: Provides the official internal job title.
Lists the top 2-3 primary metrics or KPIs by which success in this role is formally evaluated.
Differentiates between the 'official' job description and the actual lived responsibilities.
Estimates the percentage split between synchronous collaboration (meetings, calls) and asynchronous focus time.
Defines the predictability of the schedule (e.g., rigid shifts, flexible hours, on-call expectations).
States the total months/years the individual has spent in this specific role.
Lists the immediate prior role held before stepping into this position (internal or external).
Identifies the most common next-step promotion or exit opportunity for this role.
Identifies the official title of the direct manager or supervisor for this role.
Specifies the exact number of direct peers within the immediate working group or department.
Explicitly states whether the role has direct reports (Yes/No), and if Yes, quantifies how many.

subtopics.description: Main activities and deliverables in a typical week
coverage_criteria: Names at least 3 distinct primary outputs or deliverables.
Specifies whether each primary deliverable is for internal stakeholders or external clients/customers.
Provides estimated percentage breakdowns for the top tasks, summing to at least 80% of a standard week.
Explicitly categorizes core tasks by frequency (Daily, Weekly, Monthly, or Ad-hoc).

subtopics.description: Primary software tools and platforms used in daily work
coverage_criteria: Lists specific software product names rather than generic categories (e.g., 'Jira' instead of 'ticketing system').
Identifies the single most critical software tool without which the role cannot be performed.

subtopics.description: AI tools currently in use and their specific applications
coverage_criteria: Explicitly states whether generative AI or automation tools are used (Yes/No).
If Yes, describes the specific workflow stage where AI is applied (e.g., drafting, data analysis, QA).
Differentiates between company-mandated AI tools and ad-hoc/personal AI workarounds.
Lists the top 2-3 roles (not names) that this position interacts with most frequently.
Defines the dependency: identifies if this role blocks others, or is blocked by others, to complete core tasks.
Identifies the specific role(s) that assign work to this position.
Identifies the specific role(s) this position delegates work to, if any.
Outlines the standard review/approval sequence for final deliverables.
Identifies the single most time-consuming 'shadow task' or administrative burden not officially in the job description.
Categorizes the primary source of friction as either Tool-based, Process-based, or Personnel-based.
Estimates the number of hours lost per week to this specific inefficiency.

subtopics.description: Tasks or processes that feel most satisfying
coverage_criteria: Names one specific core task that consistently yields high job satisfaction.
Specifies whether the satisfaction is driven by independent problem-solving, team collaboration, or direct client/customer impact.

subtopics.description: Areas for tool or process intervention
coverage_criteria: Proposes one concrete operational change or software upgrade that would eliminate a current bottleneck.
Defines what metric would improve (e.g., speed, accuracy, employee retention) if this change were implemented.

subtopics: Recent changes that improved or worsened the work experience
